thought.text: Let me check the ASG types available

reasoning_content:
Looking at Ast2Asg.cpp, I can see that the parser is expected to produce certain AST structures. The existing code handles `TranslationUnitContext`, `DeclarationContext`, `FunctionDefinitionContext`, etc. It accesses nodes by name (e.g., `ctx->additiveExpression()`, `ctx->unaryExpression()`, etc.).

Now I need to significantly expand the parser grammar to handle all the C features needed. Let me look at what test cases we need to pass and what grammar rules are missing:

Looking at the failing test cases:
- `const` keyword usage - `010_addc.sysu.c`, `013_addc.sysu.c` etc
- `*` (star/multiply) - `017_mul.sysu.c`
- `/` (slash/divide) - `019_div.sysu.c`
- `%` (percent/mod) - `021_mod.sysu.c`
- `-` (unary minus) - `012_add2.sysu.c` is already passing though
- `if`/`else` statements - `032_if.sysu.c` etc
- `while` - `042_while_test1.sysu.c` etc
- `break`/`continue` - `045_break.sysu.c`, `046_continue.sysu.c`
- `void` - function return type
- Function calls - `030_func_defn.sysu.c`
- Comparison operators (`==`, `!=`, `<`, `>`, `<=`, `>=`)
- Logical operators (`&&`, `||`)

The task says the parser can be modified in `task/2/bison/par.y` or `task/2/antlr/SYsUParser.g4`. I'm working with antlr.

Let me update the parser grammar to support all these features. I need to be very careful to match what Ast2Asg.cpp expects.

Let me look at the asg.hpp to understand the AST node types:
